svg.text: IGBO + EFIK — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 35D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. IGBO PROVERB — ON COMMUNITY —
Onye wetara òjì wetara ndọọ.
Person who-brought kola brought life.
"He who brings kola brings life." — Kola nut is sacred in Igbo ceremony: hospitality = life-giving.
— II. IGBO PROVERB — ON MEMORY AND ELDERS —
Oke nkita anaghizi abì nà ọchọ nekǎ.
Old dog not-returns to house empty-handed.
"An old dog does not return home empty-handed." — Igbo proverb on the wisdom of elders and experience.
— III. IGBO BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
E mere ihe a mụ mụ ɔ nọ ya,
nà ndí chechee oge dì nà ịchị.
PAST make thing this without money / by people thought-about time is in heart.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who held the future in their heart." (Igbo)
— IGBO NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Oge dì nà ịchị = time that is in the heart — the future held as intention inside oneself.
Chechee = think/remember/ponder (iterative of chee = think). Aspect: ongoing pondering.
Mụ mụ (without money) = “for free”. Duplicated word for absence/lack. Vowel harmony: ụ class.
SECONDARY — EFIK — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
— I. EFIK PROVERB — ON THE RIVER AND TIME —
Ódióŋ édiókót abàkà a kèdè óbón.
The-river flows always but remembers the-moon.
"The river always flows but remembers the moon." — Efik proverb on cycles, memory, and guidance.
— II. EFIK PROVERB — ON COMMUNITY MEMORY —
Idókót mbók mbók ékè-èkè èdè mbók.
Together very-good keeping things good.
"Being together is very good — it keeps good things." (Efik communal wisdom, note reduplication mbók mbók)
— III. EFIK BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Íŋkè kè emi mmiŋ kè óyóm, mbók,
kè mmiŋ édè àkèdè ódó édió.
This was made for you freely / by people who-remembered path forward.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the road ahead." (Efik)
— EFIK NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Ódó édió = the road/path ahead — Efik uses spatial path metaphor for time: future = path forward.
Kèdè = to remember, recall. Mbók = please/freely/love (context-dependent: a rich word).
Mbók mbók (reduplication) = very much, generously — the free giving made more generous by the doubling.
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — TWO SOUTHEASTERN NIGERIAN VOICES —
Igbo: ~45M speakers. Biafra War (1967–70) devastated Igbo communities. Chinua Achebe brought it to world lit.
Efik: ~2M speakers. Calabar was Nigeria’s first colonial capital (British). First sub-Saharan Bible in Efik (1862).
Both: Niger-Congo, tonal, SVO, labial-velars. Cross River trade route connected these communities for centuries.
PLATE 35D ALT · Igbo / Efik · RUNNING TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
